title: Documented Baseline Drift — Firewall Rule Adds Outside CAB Window

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `pan:config` or syslog config events (rule, admin, cmd)
app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (Splunkbase 2757)
spl:
index=network sourcetype=pan:config earliest=-30d cmd="set" object="rule"
| lookup change_calendar.csv _time OUTPUT cab_window
| where cab_window!="open" OR isnull(cab_window)
value: Flags new allow rules created outside change windows to prove security policies embedded in change management are monitored.
implementation: (1) Normalize config sourcetype for your firewall; (2) ingest change calendar; (3) tune for automation accounts; (4) attach to policy exception workflow.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
